title: Some users' newly enrolled devices in Microsoft Intune may be unable to access company resources
impact: Users' newly enrolled devices in Microsoft Intune may be unable to access company resources.

updates.content:
Title: Some users' newly enrolled devices in Microsoft Intune may be unable to access company resources

User impact: Users' newly enrolled devices in Microsoft Intune may be unable to access company resources.

Current status: We're investigating a potential issue with Microsoft Intune and checking for impact to your organization. We'll provide an update within 30 minutes.

Title: Some users' newly enrolled devices in Microsoft Intune may be unable to access company resources

User impact: Users' newly enrolled devices in Microsoft Intune may be unable to access company resources.

More info: This issue may also impact Entra re-registered devices.

Current status: We've identified that due to a code issue, when the Microsoft Intune service attempts to calculate a device's compliance status to provide access to company resources, it's failing to propagate the device state to the underlying Entra service, resulting in impact. To recover newly enrolled devices experiencing impact, we're working to retrigger these compliance calculations to re-initiate propagation to the Entra service. In parallel, we're testing a potential code fix to ensure no further devices fall into this state.

Scope of impact: This issue may affect some users' newly enrolled devices.

Root cause: Due to a code issue, when the Microsoft Intune service attempts to calculate a device's compliance status to provide access to company resources, it's failing to propagate the device state to the underlying Entra service, resulting in impact.

Next update by: Monday, April 13, 2026, at 9:30 PM UTC

Title: Some users' newly enrolled devices in Microsoft Intune may be unable to access company resources

User impact: Users' newly enrolled devices in Microsoft Intune may be unable to access company resources.

More info: This issue may also impact Entra re-registered devices.

Current status: We've started retriggering the compliance calculations to re-initiate propagation to the Entra service for currently affected devices. Some devices may begin to see relief as we continue this action. In parallel, we've initiated the deployment of a potential code fix to our internal testing environment for validation. Once we've confirmed the fix successfully resolves the code issue as expected, we'll prepare a deployment timeline to the broader impacted environment.

Scope of impact: This issue may affect some users' newly enrolled devices.

Root cause: Due to a code issue, when the Microsoft Intune service attempts to calculate a device's compliance status to provide access to company resources, it's failing to propagate the device state to the underlying Entra service, resulting in impact.

Next update by: Tuesday, April 14, 2026, at 1:00 AM UTC
